# QuickStack Docs — Copilot Instructions

## Project overview

This is the documentation website for [QuickStack](https://github.com/biersoeckli/QuickStack), a self-hosted PaaS that deploys containerised apps onto a k3s cluster via a simple web UI.

**Stack:** Next.js (App Router) · Fumadocs MDX · Tailwind CSS 4 · shadcn/ui · Radix UI

---

## Essential commands

```bash
bun run dev          # Start dev server at http://localhost:3000
bun run build        # Production build
bun run types:check  # Run fumadocs typegen + tsc type check
bun run lint         # ESLint (eslint-config-next/core-web-vitals)
```

Run `bun run types:check` after any changes to `source.config.ts` or the `.source/` generated files.

---

## Architecture

```
content/docs/        ← All documentation content (MDX + meta.json) — edit this for doc changes
app/(home)/          ← Landing page
app/docs/            ← Docs layout + dynamic catch-all route [[...slug]]
app/api/search/      ← Full-text search API (Fumadocs)
components/landing/  ← Landing page section components
components/layout/   ← Nav, sidebar, theme/search toggles
components/ui/       ← shadcn/ui base components (do not edit unless extending)
lib/source.ts        ← Fumadocs loader — builds page tree from content/docs/
lib/layout.shared.tsx← Shared nav config (links, GitHub URL, branding)
source.config.ts     ← Fumadocs MDX collection + frontmatter schema
```

The content pipeline is: MDX files → Fumadocs MDX compile → `.source/` (generated, git-ignored) → `lib/source.ts` loader → rendered pages.

---

## Authoring documentation

### Frontmatter

Every `.mdx` file in `content/docs/` must have:

```mdx
---
title: "Page Title"
description: "Shown in search results and SEO meta tags."
---
```

Optional fields:
```mdx
---
sidebar_position: 1          # Numeric order within parent (lower = higher)
keywords: ["k1", "k2"]       # SEO keywords
---
```

### Sidebar ordering

Sidebar order is controlled by **`meta.json`** files, not by filename:

- `content/docs/meta.json` — top-level category order (`"pages": ["index", "tutorials", "how-to", "reference"]`)
- `content/docs/<category>/meta.json` — page order within a category

When adding a new page, add its filename (without `.mdx`) to the `"pages"` array in the nearest `meta.json`.

### Adding a new page

1. Create `content/docs/<category>/my-page.mdx` with the required frontmatter.
2. Add `"my-page"` to `"pages"` in `content/docs/<category>/meta.json`.
3. Run `bun run dev` to verify the page renders at `/docs/<category>/my-page`.

### Adding a new category / section

1. Create `content/docs/<new-category>/` directory.
2. Add a `meta.json` listing the pages in order.
3. Optionally add a `_category_.json` for display label and description.
4. Add `"<new-category>"` to `content/docs/meta.json`.

### Images

Place images under `public/img/docs/<topic>/`. Reference in MDX:
```mdx
![Alt text](/img/docs/my-topic/screenshot.png)
```

---

## Component conventions

- **File naming:** `kebab-case.mdx` for content, `PascalCase.tsx` for components, `camelCase.ts` for utilities.
- **Path alias:** `@/*` maps to the workspace root.
- **UI components:** Use existing shadcn components from `components/ui/`. Do not add new shadcn components unless clearly required — run `npx shadcn@latest add <component>` to add one properly.
- **Fumadocs components** (Callout, Tabs, Steps, etc.) can be used directly in MDX — see [Fumadocs UI docs](https://fumadocs.dev/docs/ui/components).

---

## Key constraints and pitfalls

- **Do not edit `.source/`** — it is auto-generated by Fumadocs MDX during build/dev. Changes are overwritten.
- **Do not edit `next-env.d.ts`** — auto-generated by Next.js.
- `source.config.ts` defines the frontmatter Zod schema. If you add a new frontmatter field, update the schema here and run `bun run types:check`.
- OG image generation uses `@takumi-rs/image-response` (listed in `serverExternalPackages`). Keep this in `next.config.mjs` if modifying that file.
- Tailwind v4 uses `@import "tailwindcss"` in CSS — no `tailwind.config.js` is present; configuration is done via CSS `@theme`.
